glossary.definition: One grouped internal stream Q[g,n] in which up to two ordered complex channels are stored as one quaternion sequence.
The fixed deterministic rule that maps ordered channel pairs (2g, 2g+1) into quaternion lanes, with explicit odd-tail handling.
The reconstruction map that reads quaternion coordinates back into the original ordered complex channels exactly.
The final unpaired complex channel when C is odd, stored alone in the last quaternion lane with zero j and k components.
The input dataset Z=(z_c[n]) where channel order is part of the contract and determines deterministic lane grouping.
The paper's scoped contribution: a lossless internal format for grouped channels before transforms, coherence metrics, or downstream inference.
The declared non-adaptive pairing convention that guarantees reproducible lift outputs for fixed channel ordering.
